The website www.janhavitraders.com (hereinafter referred to as the "Platform") is owned and operated by Janhavi Traders, a partnership firm registered under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, engaged in the wholesale and retail supply of genuine cosmetic and personal care products, including brands such as Dermalogica, Mamaearth, Nivea, and other premium beauty labels, with its principal place of business at Shop no 2, Anjanabai Appartment, Malangad Rd, near dinkar bhane nagar, Pisavli Village, Kalyan East, Kalyan, Maharashtra 421306 (hereinafter referred to as the "Company", "We", "Us", or "Our").
We value the trust you place in us. That is why We insist upon the highest standards for secure transactions and the safeguarding of your personal information. Please read this Privacy Policy carefully to understand our information-gathering and data-processing practices.
This Privacy Policy is formulated in accordance with the Information Technology Act, 2000 ("IT Act") read with the Information Technology (Reasonable Security Practices and Procedures and Sensitive Personal Data or Information) Rules, 2011 ("IT Rules"), and the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 and rules made thereunder, including any amendments. This Privacy Policy does not require any physical, electronic, or digital signature and is to be read together with the Terms and Conditions and all other policies listed on the Platform.
1. Definitions
•	"We", "Our", and "Us" shall mean and refer to Janhavi Traders, its registered partners, employees, agents, and authorised representatives.
•	"You", "Your", "Yourself", and "User" shall mean and refer to natural and legal individuals who access, browse, or transact on the Platform and avail Services for purchasing cosmetic, skincare, haircare, and personal care products.
•	"Personal Data" shall mean and refer

We shall acknowledge your grievance within 48 hours of receipt and endeavour to resolve it within 30 days from the date of receipt, in accordance with applicable law.
14. Severability
Each paragraph of this privacy policy shall be separate and independent from all other paragraphs herein. The decision or declaration that one or more of the paragraphs are null and void shall have no effect on the remaining paragraphs of this privacy policy.
15. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
This Privacy Policy shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of India. Any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or in connection with this Privacy Policy shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of competent jurisdiction located in Kalyan or Mumbai, Maharashtra, India.
Janhavi Traders | www.janhavitraders.com | janhavitraderss@gmail.com

args.CodeContent:
"# Implementation Plan - Privacy Policy Page Update

Update the website to include a premium, beautifully-designed Privacy Policy page containing the legal text provided by the user. Register the new page route and update the footer navigation.

## Proposed Changes

### Frontend

#### [NEW] [PrivacyPolicy.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/pages/PrivacyPolicy.jsx)
Create a new page component with a premium layout:
- Gradient banner header with the document overview.
- Sticky sidebar on desktop for easy navigation between the 15 sections.
- Structured card blocks/sections with smooth typography, customized list designs, and highlight boxes.
- Direct contact triggers for the Grievance Officer details.

#### [MODIFY] [App.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/App.jsx)
- Import `PrivacyPolicy` page component.
- Add route `<Route path="/privacy" element={<Layout><PrivacyPolicy /></Layout>} />` (or `/privacy-policy`).

#### [MODIFY] [Footer.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/components/layout/Footer.jsx)
- Update click handlers for 'Privacy Policy' links to navigate to `/privacy-policy`.
- Ensure scroll-to-top on navigation.

## Verification Plan

### Manual Verification
- Verify that clicking "Privacy Policy" links in the footer redirects to the new `/privacy-policy` page.
- Test responsive layout of the Privacy Policy page (sidebar collapsing into a sticky index or dropdown on mobile).
- Check styling consistency with the site's design system (`--color-primary`, `--font-display`, etc.).
"
args.Description: "Created implementation plan for Privacy Policy update"

"# Walkthrough - Privacy Policy Page Implementation

We have successfully updated the website with the new Privacy Policy page and linked it to the footer.

## Changes Made

### 1. Created the Privacy Policy Page
- Created a new component at [PrivacyPolicy.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/pages/PrivacyPolicy.jsx) featuring:
  - An interactive, responsive sidebar acting as a sticky table of contents on desktop layouts.
  - A scrollspy setup that dynamically highlights the active section in the sidebar as the user scrolls.
  - Premium styled components including:
    - Ambient rose/gold gradient background accents.
    - Custom styled lists for Definitions and Use of Information.
    - Custom Alert cards for Cash on Delivery policies and Security practices.
    - A contact card widget for the Grievance Officer details.

### 2. Registered Route in Router
- Updated [App.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/App.jsx) to import `PrivacyPolicy` and register the route `/privacy-policy` inside the customer Layout.

### 3. Integrated Links in Footer
- Updated [Footer.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/components/layout/Footer.jsx) to link both the "Privacy Policy" button in the Help column and the bottom-most footer bar link directly to the `/privacy-policy` route.

## Verification

### Build Validation
- Ran the production build script locally to ensure compilation passes without errors:
  ```bash
  npm run build
  ```
  **Result:** The build compiled cleanly in 655ms:
  ```text
  dist/index.html                   0.58 kB
  dist/assets/index-1vMruZ45.css   32.47 kB
  dist/assets/index-DFsTW_vN.js   487.50 kB
  ✓ built in 655ms
  ```
"

JANHAVI TRADER
RETURN & CANCELLATION POLICY
www.janhavitraders.com
Document Type	E-Commerce Return, Refund & Cancellation Policy
Effective Date	June 11, 2026
Operational Model	Exclusive Cash on Delivery (COD) with Open Box Delivery

Overview
This Return & Cancellation Policy ("Policy") governs all purchase transactions made through the website www.janhavitraders.com (the "Platform") operated by Janhavi Traders, a partnership firm registered under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, based in Maharashtra, India.
To protect our consumers and maintain strict hygiene standards for cosmetic and personal care products, Janhavi Traders operates on an Exclusive Cash on Delivery (COD) model backed by a mandatory Open Box Delivery framework. This Policy applies to both retail customers and wholesale / B2B buyers. By completing a purchase on the Platform, you agree to be bound by the terms of this Policy.
1. Mandatory Open Box Delivery Policy
To ensure absolute transparency, all orders shipped via www.janhavitraders.com are fulfilled via Open Box Delivery.
•	The Inspection Process: At the time of delivery, the customer must open the outer shipping package and inspect the items inside in the presence of our logistics delivery partner before handing over the cash payment.
•	Doorstep Rejection: If you notice that a product is physically damaged, leaking, tampered with, or if a wrong variant/shade or incorrect item has been brought to you, you have the right to reject the shipment at the doorstep immediately.
•	Financial Settlement: Since our platform is strictly COD, if you reject the package at the doorstep, no transaction occurs. You are not required to pay any cash, and no refund processing is necessary.
•	Post-Acceptance Waiver: Once you inspect the package, accept it, and hand over the cash to the courier partner, you explicitly waive the right to claim returns or refunds based on transit damage, physical breakage, cosmetic leakage, or an incomplete
<truncated 3848 bytes>
diately by the delivery handler at the doorstep. No post-delivery claims for volume shortages or breakage will be entertained after the bulk shipment has been accepted and paid for.
6. Contact & Support Escalation
For queries related to order timelines, delivery rejections, or post-delivery defect assistance, contact us exclusively through our support channels:

•	Primary Support Email: janhavitraderss@gmail.com
•	Official Phone Support / WhatsApp: +91 84338 93228 / +91 89287 62528
•	Principal Place of Business: Shop no 2, Anjanabai Appartment, Malangad Rd, near dinkar bhane nagar, Pisavli Village, Kalyan East, Kalyan, Maharashtra 421306
•	Grievance Officer Redressal Escalation: Complaints that are not resolved at the support level can be escalated directly to our Grievance Officer, Rohit Jaiswar, at the contact details published within our official Privacy Policy framework.
7. Changes to This Policy
Janhavi Traders reserves the right to revise, amend, or update this Return & Cancellation Policy at any time. All updates will be published on the Platform with a revised effective date.
Janhavi Traders  |  www.janhavitraders.com  |  janhavitraderss@gmail.com

"# Implementation Plan - Return & Cancellation Policy Page Update

Update the website to include a premium, beautifully-designed Return & Cancellation Policy page containing the legal and operational details provided by the user. Register the new page route and update the footer navigation.

## Proposed Changes

### Frontend

#### [NEW] [ReturnPolicy.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/pages/ReturnPolicy.jsx)
Create a new page component with a premium layout:
- Gradient banner header with document overview (Operational model: COD with Open Box Delivery).
- Sticky sidebar on desktop for navigating the 7 sections of the policy.
- Beautiful custom tables/grid layouts for the post-delivery return eligibility grid (Hidden Defects).
- Styling that matches the existing design system (`--color-primary`, Playfair Display, and Inter fonts).
- Responsive layout with collapsible sections/sidebar on mobile.

#### [MODIFY] [App.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/App.jsx)
- Import `ReturnPolicy` page component.
- Add route `<Route path="/returns-policy" element={<Layout><ReturnPolicy /></Layout>} />` (or `/returns-exchange`).

#### [MODIFY] [Footer.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/components/layout/Footer.jsx)
- Update click handler for the 'Returns & Exchange' button in the Help column to navigate to `/returns-policy`.

## Verification Plan

### Manual Verification
- Verify that clicking "Returns & Exchange" in the Help section of the footer redirects to the new `/returns-policy` page.
- Test responsiveness of the return page layout (table and grid scaling, mobile spacing).
- Verify consistency of design tokens (rose colors, card layouts, table typography).
"

Update the website to include a premium, beautifully-designed Return & Cancellation Policy page containing the legal and operational details provided by the user. Register the new page route and update the footer navigation.

## Proposed Changes

### Frontend

#### [NEW] [ReturnPolicy.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/pages/ReturnPolicy.jsx)
Create a new page component with a premium layout:
- Gradient banner header with document overview (Operational model: COD with Open Box Delivery).

- Beautiful custom tables/grid layouts for the post-delivery return eligibility grid (Hidden Defects).
- Styling that matches the existing design system (`--color-primary`, Playfair Display, and Inter fonts).
- Responsive layout with collapsible sections/sidebar on mobile.

#### [MODIFY] [App.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/App.jsx)

## Verification Plan

"# Walkthrough - Policy Pages Implementation

We have successfully updated the website with both the **Privacy Policy** and the **Return & Cancellation Policy** pages, and linked them to the footer.

## Changes Made

### 1. Created the Privacy Policy Page
- Created a new component at [PrivacyPolicy.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/pages/PrivacyPolicy.jsx) featuring:
  - An interactive, responsive sidebar acting as a sticky table of contents on desktop layouts.
  - A scrollspy setup that dynamically highlights the active section in the sidebar as the user scrolls.
  - Premium styled components with ambient rose/gold gradient background accents, custom lists, and a Grievance Officer card.

### 2. Created the Return & Cancellation Policy Page
- Created a new component at [ReturnPolicy.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/pages/ReturnPolicy.jsx) featuring:
  - Layout structure consistent with the Privacy Policy page.
  - Sticky table of contents sidebar on desktop.
  - A responsive eligibility status table for Post-Delivery returns (Hidden Defects vs Change of mind).
  - Clear section layout cards for cancellation scenarios (Before Dispatch, In-Transit, Doorstep COD).
  - Ambient styling with custom cards for Refund options (Cash refund vs NEFT bank transfer) and support channels.

### 3. Registered Routes in Router
- Updated [App.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/App.jsx) to import both pages and register the routes:
  - `/privacy-policy` for `PrivacyPolicy`
  - `/returns-policy` for `ReturnPolicy`

### 4. Integrated Links in Footer
- Updated [Footer.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/components/layout/Footer.jsx) to link:
  - "Privacy Policy" button in the Help column and at the bottom bar to `/privacy-policy`.
  - "Returns & Exchange" button in the Help column to `/returns-policy`.

## Verification

#

args.CodeContent:
"# Implementation Plan - Terms of Service Page Update

Update the website to include a premium, beautifully-designed Terms of Service page containing the legal terms provided by the user. Register the new page route and update the footer navigation.

## Proposed Changes

### Frontend

#### [NEW] [TermsOfService.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/pages/TermsOfService.jsx)
Create a new page component with a premium layout:
- Gradient banner header with the document overview.
- Sticky sidebar on desktop for navigating the 10 sections of the Terms.
- Customized blocks for user eligibility (open access policy), exclusive COD terms, and the open box delivery protocol.
- Styling that matches the existing design system (`--color-primary`, Playfair Display, and Inter fonts).
- Responsive layout with collapsible sections/sidebar on mobile.

#### [MODIFY] [App.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/App.jsx)
- Import `TermsOfService` page component.
- Add route `<Route path="/terms-of-service" element={<Layout><TermsOfService /></Layout>} />`.

#### [MODIFY] [Footer.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/components/layout/Footer.jsx)
- Update click handler for the bottom "Terms of Service" button to navigate to `/terms-of-service`.

## Verification Plan

### Manual Verification
- Verify that clicking "Terms of Service" in the bottom of the footer redirects to `/terms-of-service`.
- Test responsive layout of the Terms page (sidebar collapsing or hidden on tablet/mobile).
- Verify consistency of design tokens (primary rose color, card styling, typography).
"

    - Post-Delivery defect report forms (within 24 hours).

### 4. Registered Routes in Router

Welcome to Janhavi Traders! We are committed to providing you with 100% genuine premium cosmetics, skincare, haircare, and personal care supplies.

thinking:
**Analyzing the Font Conflict**

I've pinpointed a font size conflict in the `index.css` file. The `.navbar-logo` on desktop uses `font-size: 1.4rem`. On mobile, it's colliding with the logo sizing and padding. It looks like the `1.4rem` font combined with the logo and gap creates a display overflow, causing the conflict. I need to figure out the fix.


**Calculating the Overflow Issue**

I've calculated the total width the logo, text, buttons, and padding consume, which is around 446px. This explains the overflow on smaller screens. I also looked closer at the screenshot and realized the search icon is directly on top of the "TRADERS" text, which means it is an overflow issue with how the `.navbar-inner` element is arranged, as `.navbar-inner` has `display: flex`.

I have resolved the collision between the search icon and the brand name in the mobile view of the navbar, and successfully pushed the changes to your remote repository.

### What Was Fixed:
1. **Logo Font-Size:** Added a CSS media query in [index.css](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/index.css#L312-L317) that scales down the font size of the `JANHAVI TRADERS` text from `1.4rem` to `1.15rem` on screens smaller than `768px` (mobile layout).
2. **Hidden redundant buttons:** Added a general `.hidden-mobile` utility class to [index.css](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/index.css#L212) and applied it to the User/Account icon button wrapper in [Navbar.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/components/layout/Navbar.jsx#L134). Since the User/Account links are already fully available inside the mobile hamburger drawer menu, hiding this icon on mobile frees up valuable layout space to prevent any overlapping.

Yes, the **Google Sign-in** feature is fully set up and correct in both the frontend and backend code, but it is currently **inactive** because the Google OAuth Client ID has not yet been configured in the environment settings.

### Current Implementation Status:
- **Frontend Code ([Login.jsx](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx#L49-L77)):** Checks for `VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`. If present, it initializes the Google Identity SDK (which is loaded in `index.html`), renders the official Google sign-in button, and posts the resulting credentials to `/auth/google/`.
- **Backend Code ([views.py](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/backend/users/views.py#L291-L355)):** Receives the credential token, validates it against Google's public tokeninfo verification endpoints, automatically creates/signs-in the user, updates their avatar picture and name, and issues JWT access tokens.

---

### How to Activate and Test Google Sign-in:

To run it, you need a Client ID from your Google Cloud Console.

1. **Get Client ID from Google Cloud Console:**
   - Go to the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/).
   - Create a project (or select an existing one) and navigate to **APIs & Services > Credentials**.
   - Create an **OAuth 2.0 Client ID** for a **Web Application**.
   - Add your test URLs (e.g. `http://localhost:5173` or `https://www.janhavitraders.com`) to both **Authorized JavaScript origins** and **Authorized redirect URIs**.

2. **Configure Frontend:**
   - Open [frontend/.env](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/frontend/.env).
   - Set the `VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` variable:
     ```env
     VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=your-google-client-id-here.apps.googleusercontent.com
     ```
   - Restart your Vite frontend server so it loads the new environment variable.

3. **Configure Backend (Optional but Recommended for Audience Verification):**
   - In your backend environment config (`.env` or similar), set `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` to the same Client ID.
   - If not set on the backend, the sign-in will still succeed (skipping audience matching verification to simplify testing).

args.CodeContent:
"# 🔍 Janhavi Traders — Full Website Analysis

**Website**: www.janhavitraders.com  
**Stack**: React (Vite) frontend on Vercel + Django REST backend on Render (Gunicorn + PostgreSQL)  
**Date**: June 14, 2026

---

## Architecture Overview

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph Frontend ["Frontend (Vercel)"]
        A["React SPA (Vite)"]
    end
    subgraph Backend ["Backend (Render)"]
        B["Django REST + Gunicorn (2 workers)"]
        C["PostgreSQL (Render)"]
        D["Cloudinary CDN (Images)"]
    end
    A -->|HTTPS API calls| B
    B --> C
    B --> D
    A -->|Google GSI Script| E["Google OAuth"]
    B -->|Token verify| E
    B -->|Email| F["Resend / Brevo API"]
    B -->|SMS| G["Fast2SMS API"]
```

| Layer | Technology | Hosting |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| Frontend | React 19, Vite 8, Zustand, React Router 7 | Vercel (Free tier) |
| Backend | Django 4.2, DRF, SimpleJWT, Gunicorn | Render (Free/Starter) |
| Database | PostgreSQL (via `dj-database-url`) | Render Managed DB |
| Media/CDN | Cloudinary | Cloudinary Free |
| Email | Resend / Brevo HTTPS API | Third-party |
| SMS | Fast2SMS | Third-party |

---

## 🐛 Bugs Found

### BUG-1: Race Condition in Order Stock Decrement (🔴 Critical)

**File**: [views.py](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/backend/orders/views.py#L73-L86)

The `PlaceOrderView` decrements `stock_qty` **without database-level locking**. Two users buying the same product simultaneously can both read the same stock value, both succeed, and drive stock negative.

```python
# Current (Unsafe):
item.product.stock_qty = max(0, item.product.stock_qty - item.quantity)
item.product.save(update_fields=['stock_qty'])
```

**Fix**: Use `F()` expressions for atomic updates or `select_for_update()`:
```python
from django.db.models import F
Product.objects.filter(pk=item.product.pk).update(
    stock_qty=Greatest(F('stock_qty') - item.quantity, 0)
)
```

## 🔍 Analysis Complete

I've created a comprehensive **website analysis report** covering your entire stack. Here are the key findings:

### 🐛 8 Bugs Found
- **BUG-1 (Critical)**: Race condition in stock decrement — two simultaneous orders can oversell
- **BUG-3 (Medium)**: "Deal of the Day" links to `/products/{id}` instead of `/products/{slug}` — leads to 404
- **BUG-5 (Critical)**: OTP codes are printed to production logs — security risk
- **BUG-8 (Low)**: Newsletter "Subscribe" button does nothing

### 🔒 10 Security Vulnerabilities
- **CORS is wide open** (`CORS_ALLOW_ALL_ORIGINS = True`) — any site can call your API
- **No rate limiting on OTP** — attackers can brute-force codes or flood your SMS credits
- **SECRET_KEY has insecure hardcoded fallback**
- **`.env` file is committed to repo**

### 📊 Concurrent User Capacity
| Scenario | Max Users |
|----------|-----------|
| Casual browsing | ~100 |
| Active shopping | ~30 |
| Peak checkout | **~10-15** |

> Your **primary bottleneck is 2 Gunicorn workers**. Adding `--workers 4 --threads 2` to your Procfile would roughly double capacity at zero cost.

### 🚀 Top 3 Immediate Actions
1. Fix `CORS_ALLOW_ALL_ORIGINS` → restrict to your domain only
2. Add OTP rate limiting → prevent SMS/email API abuse
3. Increase Gunicorn workers → `--workers 4 --threads 2`

Check the full report artifact for detailed code references, fix examples, and a scaling roadmap from current capacity to 1000+ users.

"# 🔍 Janhavi Traders — Full Website Analysis

**Website**: www.janhavitraders.com  
**Stack**: React (Vite) frontend on Vercel + Django REST backend on Render (Gunicorn + PostgreSQL)  
**Date**: June 14, 2026

---

## Architecture Overview

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph Frontend ["Frontend (Vercel)"]
        A["React SPA (Vite)"]
    end
    subgraph Backend ["Backend (Render)"]
        B["Django REST + Gunicorn (2 workers)"]
        C["PostgreSQL (Render)"]
        D["Cloudinary CDN (Images)"]
    end
    A -->|HTTPS API calls| B
    B --> C
    B --> D
    A -->|Google GSI Script| E["Google OAuth"]
    B -->|Token verify| E
    B -->|Email| F["Resend / Brevo API"]
    B -->|SMS| G["Fast2SMS API"]
```

| Layer | Technology | Hosting |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| Frontend | React 19, Vite 8, Zustand, React Router 7 | Vercel (Free tier) |
| Backend | Django 4.2, DRF, SimpleJWT, Gunicorn | Render (Free/Starter) |
| Database | PostgreSQL (via `dj-database-url`) | Render Managed DB |
| Media/CDN | Cloudinary | Cloudinary Free |
| Email | Resend / Brevo HTTPS API | Third-party |
| SMS | Fast2SMS | Third-party |

---

## 🐛 Bugs Found

### BUG-1: Race Condition in Order Stock Decrement (🔴 Critical)

**File**: [views.py](file:///c:/Users/sahil/Desktop/janhavi%20traders/busy-maxwell/backend/orders/views.py#L73-L86)

The `PlaceOrderView` decrements `stock_qty` **without database-level locking**. Two users buying the same product simultaneously can both read the same stock value, both succeed, and drive stock negative.

```python
# Current (Unsafe):
item.product.stock_qty = max(0, item.product.stock_qty - item.quantity)
item.product.save(update_fields=['stock_qty'])
```

**Fix**: Use `F()` expressions for atomic updates or `select_for_update()`:
```python
from django.db.models import F
Product.objects.filter(pk=item.product.pk).update(
    stock_qty=Greatest(F('stock_qty') - item.quantity, 0)
)
```

---

### BUG-2: SKU Coll
<truncated 14524 bytes>

## 🔍 Analysis Complete

I've created a comprehensive **website analysis report** covering your entire stack. Here are the key findings:

### 🐛 8 Bugs Found
- **BUG-1 (Critical)**: Race condition in stock decrement — two simultaneous orders can oversell
- **BUG-3 (Medium)**: "Deal of the Day" links to `/products/{id}` instead of `/products/{slug}` — leads to 404
- **BUG-5 (Critical)**: OTP codes are printed to production logs — security risk
- **BUG-8 (Low)**: Newsletter "Subscribe" button does nothing

### 🔒 10 Security Vulnerabilities
- **CORS is wide open** (`CORS_ALLOW_ALL_ORIGINS = True`) — any site can call your API
- **No rate limiting on OTP** — attackers can brute-force codes or flood your SMS credits
- **SECRET_KEY has insecure hardcoded fallback**
- **`.env` file is committed to repo**

### 📊 Concurrent User Capacity
| Scenario | Max Users |
|----------|-----------|
| Casual browsing | ~100 |
| Active shopping | ~30 |
| Peak checkout | **~10-15** |

> Your **primary bottleneck is 2 Gunicorn workers**. Adding `--workers 4 --threads 2` to your Procfile would roughly double capacity at zero cost.

### 🚀 Top 3 Immediate Actions
1. Fix `CORS_ALLOW_ALL_ORIGINS` → restrict to your domain only
2. Add OTP rate limiting → prevent SMS/email API abuse
3. Increase Gunicorn workers → `--workers 4 --threads 2`

### Bug Fixes and Code Quality Enhancements Report

Here is a summary of the bug fixes, security hardening, and code quality improvements applied to the Janhavi Traders repository:

1. **BUG-01 (Deal of Day Route Parameter Fix)** in  rontend/src/pages/Home.jsx:
   - **Change**: Redispatched the \'Grab this Offer\' click handler from id to slug.
   - **Reason**: The product details route uses slug parameters (/products/:slug) rather than database primary keys. Clicking the button previously triggered a 404 error page.

2. **BUG-02 (OTP Logging Security Hardening)** in  ackend/users/views.py:
   - **Change**: Guarded OTP printing statements to the console under settings.DEBUG == True.
   - **Reason**: Printing raw user OTP credentials to the server console in production represents a severe security risk.

3. **BUG-03 (Order Stock Decrement Race Condition)** in  ackend/orders/views.py:
   - **Change**: Replaced standard read-modify-write decrementing with Django atomic F() database-level update expressions (Greatest(F(\'stock_qty\') - item.quantity, 0)).
   - **Reason**: Simultaneous checkout operations on the same item previously suffered from race conditions, potentially leading to stock overselling and negative quantities.

4. **BUG-04 (OTP Request Throttling)** in  ackend/janhavi_backend/settings/base.py and  ackend/users/views.py:
   - **Change**: Configured OTPRateThrottle and registered a throttle rate of 3/minute for the otp scope.
   - **Reason**: Prevented brute force/guessing attacks on OTPs and resource/financial exhaustion of external SMS/email APIs.

5. **BUG-05 (CORS Security Restriction)** in  ackend/janhavi_backend/settings/base.py:
   - **Change**: Set CORS_ALLOW_ALL_ORIGINS = False and restricted origins strictly to https://janhavitraders.com (and localhost inside DEBUG mode).
   - **Reason**: Exposing wildcard CORS headers in production allows any third-party malicious website to execute cross-site queries on behalf of users.

6. **BUG-06 (SECRET_KEY Security Hardening)** in  ackend/janhavi_backend/settings/base.py:
   - **Change**: Removed the insecure default secret key fallback.
   - **Reason**: Prompts Django to fail to boot if a production-grade SECRET_KEY environment variable is not explicitly supplied.

7. **BUG-07 (OTP Dynamic Expiry)** in  ackend/users/models.py:
   - **Change**: Loaded expiration minutes from settings using getattr(settings, \'OTP_EXPIRY_MINUTES\', 10).
   - **Reason**: Allowed changing OTP validity dynamically using Django configurations rather than maintaining a hardcoded value in models.

8. **BUG-08/BUG-09 (Dynamic Categories loading)** in  rontend/src/components/layout/Navbar.jsx:
   - **Change**: Loaded categories dynamically via API GET call /products/categories/, fallback to static default arrays on failure.
   - **Reason**: Navbar links are now dynamically managed from the database rather than hardcoded.

9. **BUG-09 (Checkout clearCart Async Mismatch)** in  rontend/src/store/cartStore.js:
   - **Change**: Converted clearCart() to asynchronous method calling DELETE /cart/ API.
   - **Reason**: Aligned clearCart with Checkout.jsx usage where it was awaited, making sure cart clears on the backend database synchronously during checkout.

10. **BUG-10 (Google Sign-In Stale Closure)** in  rontend/src/pages/Login.jsx:
    - **Change**: Wrapped Google response handler inside a mutable useRef reference.
    - **Reason**: Prevented stale closure issues where asynchronous Google initialization captured old component states.

11. **BUG-12 (MyOrders Pagination)** in  rontend/src/pages/MyOrders.jsx:
    - **Change**: Added pagination states (page, hasNext, hasPrev, 	otal) and rendered Previous/Next pagination buttons.
    - **Reason**: Users can now browse lists of orders exceeding the page chunk size (20 items).

12. **BUG-13 (React Error Boundary)** in  rontend/src/App.jsx:
    - **Change**: Created custom Class-based ErrorBoundary rendering a clean fallback UI and wrapped application <Routes> with it.
    - **Reason**: Any client-side routing crash will display a friendly reload option rather than turning the screen completely white.

### Created Admin Superuser Account

- **Email**: janhavitraderss@gmail.com
- **Permissions**: is_staff = True, is_superuser = True (Full access to both frontend admin interface and Django admin panel).
- **Reason**: Enabled the website administrator to log in to the custom frontend admin panel via OTP/Google Sign-In, and to log in to the backend Django administration panel (/admin) using their email and static password.

### Created/Updated Production Admin Superuser Account

- **Email**: janhavitraderss@gmail.com
- **Permissions**: is_staff = True, is_superuser = True (Full access to both frontend admin interface and Django admin panel on production website).
- **Reason**: Enabled the website administrator to log in to the custom frontend admin panel via OTP/Google Sign-In, and to log in to the backend Django administration panel (/admin) using their email and static password in the live production database.
- **Database connected**: Singapore-hosted PostgreSQL Render DB (janhavidb).

### Connection Integrity Analysis Report

I completed a comprehensive check of all infrastructure, database, CORS, and external integration connection paths for both local development and live production setups.

1. **Backend-to-Database Connection**:
   - **Local status**: Active & healthy (SQLite file db.sqlite3 parsed successfully).
   - **Production status**: Active & healthy (successfully tested connecting to Singapore-hosted PostgreSQL DB via external URL). Query, write, and delete transactions all executed successfully.

2. **Frontend-to-Backend Connection**:
   - **Local status**: Active & healthy (Vite client successfully resolves requests to local API at http://localhost:8000/api).
   - **Production status**: Fully functional provided that the frontend Vercel environment variable VITE_API_URL is set to the Django Web Service URL (e.g., https://janhavi-backend.onrender.com), not the direct PostgreSQL database URL.

3. **CORS Security Limits**:
   - **Local status**: Correctly allows localhost domains (localhost:5173, etc.).
   - **Production status**: Restricted to official domains https://janhavitraders.com and https://www.janhavitraders.com. Any other domains will be blocked by CORS unless explicitly registered in CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS inside  ase.py.
